name: Shige's Saimin Stand
tagline: Townies willingly make the 30-minute drive to Wahiawa because nobody else makes saimin noodles in-house like Shige's does.
talkStory: Opened in 1990 by Shige Tamashiro in a Wahiawa strip mall, this is one of the last spots on Oahu making saimin noodles from scratch daily. The handmade noodles have a chewier, silkier texture you won't find anywhere else. Locals drive 30+ minutes from town specifically for this. The teri cheeseburger is a sleeper hit — get it as a combo with saimin. Featured on Hawaii's Best multiple times.
